programme.desc: Three of the North Easts finest chefs, Michelin trained Paul Welburn, Michelin starred Frances Atkins and returning champion Colin McGurran will cook their menus for the mystery veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Michelin starred Frances Atkins, determined Paul Welburn and returning champion Colin McGurran go head to head with their fish courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Frances Atkins, Paul Welburn and Colin McGurran attempt to impress the veteran judge with their main courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with their desserts as only two will get to cook for the judges. Frances Atkins serves another dish inspired by her fathers involvement in D Day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs for the North East fight it out for one place in the finals, cooking all four of their courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis must do everything they can to impress the surprise veteran judge wholl be scoring their dishes with their starter courses and theyre each drawing on personal inspiration.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, Jason Athertons protege James Durrant and fellow newcomer Mark Ellis go head to head for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, and after the disastrous fish course Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis are all desperate to impress the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis last chance to impress with their desserts as at the end of todays cooking one chef must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort, who must decide which chef will win a place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of London and the South Easts superstar chefs, returning contender Adam Simmonds, Michelin-starred newcomer Tom Sellers and his former boss Adam Byatt, attempt to dazzle the mystery judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: London and South East chefs, Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt continue their battle. Does a trip to Adam Byatts nans house to find out about his grandfathers military connections spur him on to fish course glory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt go head to head cooking their main courses. At the crucial half way stage the chefs must do all they can to score highly.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course time as the London and South East chefs compete for one of two places in the judges chamber and the competition couldnt be tighter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a tightly fought battle today as the two remaining London and South East chefs cook all four of their dishes again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge, Joy Hunter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, who will help decide which chef will go forward to the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the south wests finest chefs - Jude Kereama, Josh Eggleton and Dominic Chapman - attempt to impress a veteran judge with their starters. As the only newcomer in the kitchen, Jude is determined to plate up a winning dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three south west chefs fight it out for the fish course. Despite schoolboy errors during the starter, experienced Dominic is determined to get a high score on the course. But will newcomer Jude, a seafood master, overcome his nerves to plate up?.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course as the three south-west chefs go head to head. Newcomer Jude is brimming with determination and confidence as the main course battle hits the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The south-west chefs have one last chance to impress as they face the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges; with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining south-west judges must cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Joining the judges is Radhika Bynon, a member of the Forest Gate Womens Institute, a modern urban group of women.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael are challenged to plate up perfect starter courses for this weeks veteran judge. Its the first time in the Great British Menu kitchen for all three chefs, so who will triumph and who will fall?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael fight it out for the fish course. Experimental chef Stephen is determined to prove himself with a fish course honouring the WIs tradition of preserving, but hes up against stiff competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with the Welsh chefs going head to head. With all three chefs paying homage to Welsh lamb; Adam with a refined faggot, Phil with a dish dedicated to his Mum and Stephen with a lamb dish celebrating the WI.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the tricky dessert course and the last chance for the Welsh chefs to secure their place in tomorrows regional final. With everything to play for, the pressure is fierce. Only two can make it through, and one will leave the competition for good.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two remaining Welsh chefs cook their four courses again for the judges and Rosemary Bishton. One chef will be chosen to represent Wales in the National Finals taking them a step closer to cooking at the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Northern Irelands top chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold - go head to head with their starter courses to impress this weeks veteran judge. Former champion Chris is back for glory and determined to reach the banquet, but hes up against two newcomers with their sights firmly set on victory.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold are cooking their fish courses. Ambitious Ben is determined to steal Chriss regional crown, but under fierce scrutiny from this weeks formidable veteran, will the pressure to deliver spell melt down?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of Northern Irelands finest chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold. Ambitious Ben is out to prove himself with a perfect lamb main course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Northern Irish chefs are all striving for one of the two spots in the Regional Final. The pressure is intense, and in the end one chef must leave the competition empty handed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North Irish chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - award winning food writer Felicity Cloake.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen are going head to head this week for a chance to represent the North East region in the National Finals. Today its a battle for starter course, and the first chance to impress their formidable veteran chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fight for the fish course, as three of the North Easts finest chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - are out to impress. After his risky starter caused a stir yesterday Michael is planning another controversial dish in honour of the WIs support of womens rights.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the North Easts most dynamic chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - go head to head cooking their main courses. Michelin starred Tim is super keen to bag the first ten of the week, but Michael and Mini are hot on his heels.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with the dessert course. Its been a week of highs and lows, modern audacious dishes and classic masterpieces but with only two places up for grabs cooking for the judges, at the end of today who will leave the competition empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - WI president Kirsty Bowen from Sheffield - who will help decide which chef will go through to represent the North East in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Representing the North West are Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and returning contender Mark Ellis. Todays the starter course, and after failing to cook for the judges last year Marks out to redeem himself but he faces stiff competition in executive chef Eve and young gun Matt.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course, as three of the North Wests finest chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis - go head to head. Eve and Matt are hoping their classic approach to cooking fish will win the day whilst Mark is going all out with fishy take on a traditional dessert recipe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the main course for the three North West chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis. Mark remains determined and focused on his battle to get to judges chamber this year, but as the pressure to plate up perfection reaches fever pitch, will all three chefs get a dish to the pass?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North West chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow. Its the end of the road for one chef, but who will it be?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by award winning food writer Sabrina Ghayour and together theyll decide which chef will go on to represent the North West in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course for three of London and the South Easts hottest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt Gillan is determined to make it to this years banquet, but he faces fierce competition in the two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund - go head to head with their fish courses. Lee is hoping his modern plate of mackerel and passion fruit will outclass Matt and Marks more traditionally inspired salmon dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of London and the South Easts finest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt is determined to bring home a 10 with his ambitious celebration of the billy goat but has he bitten off more than he can chew?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the London and South East chefs are striving to secure one of the two spots in tomorrows Regional Final. The scores are close, so its all to play for, as today marks the end of the competition for one man.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and South East chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by Mary Gwynn, author of the official WI centenary cookbook. Together theyll decide which chef goes through to represent the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central Regions finest chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge with their starters. With big boots to fill, Angela Hartnetts protege Pip is the only newcomer in the kitchen. Will she beat the boys to claim victory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central Region chefs - newcomer Pip Lacey, 4th time competitor Richard Bainbridge and returning chef Jason Hodnett - fight it out for the fish course. Pip is hoping to outclass her rivals with a smoked haddock dish inspired by her Grandma.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with three Central Region chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett. Once again Jason is taking risks with his unusual collection of ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the Central Region chefs to impress, with the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges so with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central Region chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyll be joined by former chair of the Womens Institute Helen Carey, who will help decide who will have the glory of representing the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course, and 8 of the finest chefs in the country are battling it out to plate up impeccable dishes worthy of the Womens Institute. The judges are joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, but after Oliver reveals that this year it will be much tougher to make it onto the judges shortlist, who will keep their cool and secure a place?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the fish course, and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by a WI president, Kristy Bowen, who knows the impeccable standards the Institute will expect.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, the half-way point in Finals Week, and those chefs who have yet to get a dish on the shortlist are feeling the heat. With only dishes judged to be perfect in with a chance at reaching the WI banquet, the standards are higher than ever before.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for dessert, and the last chance for the 8 regional champions to get a dish in contention for the final banquet. Those whove failed to make the judges shortlist so far are going all out. Joining the judges is author of the WI centenary cookbook Mary Gwynn.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The banquet is finally here. Its the culmination of months of hard work as the winning chefs strive to plate up perfect dishes for the ladies of the Womens Institute, at Londons historic Drapers Hall where pioneers of the WI once gathered.

programme.desc: Suggs discovers the history of the British love of the humble hostelry and explores a load of interesting issues along the way, from womens role in brewing, and the community role of the pub, to the pub as a place of literary and musical creativity.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: The Freemasons have been meeting since the 14th century. From an ancient stoneworkers guild through to the modern era, their mythologies, beliefs, and secret rituals have inspired countless works of fiction and speculation.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the citys story.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From a hidden tomb in Turkey to a death bed in Norway, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding the Karun Treasure, Edvard Munchs The Sick Child paintings and more. (S1, ep 9)

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: Archaeologists and scientists help to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the worlds most fascinating treasures.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Viking Camp And Iron Age Gold
programme.desc: A look at the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and groundbreaking evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isnt just a sacred site but a place where people lived and worked. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look at the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, a dive into the English Channel reveals the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose, an 18th-century East India Company ship packed with silver.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In York, Professor Alice Roberts takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds, and gets her hands on Viking poo.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britains most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Find out how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8,000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Join a team at a secret location in Dorset as they excavate an Iron Age crouched burial and in Londons East End,location reporter Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: In Cambodia, Asia, lies the largest religious building ever built by man: the temple of Angkor Wat. Covering 160 hectares, it is the largest of the 112 Khmer temples lost in 400 km2 of jungle.

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: In Turkey, on the border between Europe and Asia, stands a remarkable marvel of ancient engineering: Istanbuls Hagia Sophia. It is a colossal building. Built in the sixth century, it has been the largest monument for almost a thousand years.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Battle of Culloden: Rick and Beau return to tackle new rivers in a brand-new series. From Wales to Scotland, the pair investigate the cold and murky waters of historic sites. (1 of 3)

programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miners cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctors surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughters plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Theres a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers visit Rutland Luxury Lodges in Greetham, owned by Ruth and Lucy Hinch. The guests find luxury in abundance, but Jackie and Nigel get hot and bothered about the bedding.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Oakover Guest House in Weston-super-Mare, hosted by Simon Robinson and Ann Wilson. The BandBers are delighted by the decor, but Lucy and Ruth get a frosty reception...

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Abby hit her head after falling from the steps of a high diving board. The medical team need to scan her head and neck to check for serious injuries. Meanwhile, the hospital security team deals with over 50 incidents every month in AandE, ranging from verbal abuse to assault. Anne and Holton are both working the night shift, dealing with a variety of challenging patients in the hospital.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: This episode takes a moving - and sometimes light-hearted - look at when boys become men, and passing the baton between the generations. Twenty-six-year-old Nicholas has been punched in the face in a random attack. Meanwhile, Tyrell has damaged his big toe playing football, and Ho has brought his 78-year-old grandma Amoui to Kings after finding her collapsed in her flat.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line

programme.desc: On the last night in Bournemouth, flamboyant charity manager Jacqueline hopes that Michelin star-style food will win her the prize.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Grange Boutique Hotel in Cumbria, Jackie and Nigel hope to impress with high-end suites. But Simon finds his room stifling and the breakfast eggs arent all theyre cracked up to be.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers visit Rutland Luxury Lodges in Greetham, owned by Ruth and Lucy Hinch. The guests find luxury in abundance, but Jackie and Nigel get hot and bothered about the bedding.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Leicester Arms in Kent, hosted by husbands Laurence and Danny Bowes. Some guests get a spotless welcome, but Jackie and Nigel make dirty discoveries.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Oakover Guest House in Weston-super-Mare, hosted by Simon Robinson and Ann Wilson. The BandBers are delighted by the decor, but Lucy and Ruth get a frosty reception...

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its feedback time as the BandBers meet to complain about breakfast eggs, dirty rooms and sleepless nights. One owners strategy comes under scrutiny, before the winner is announced.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend take on a 1980s legend, a British championship-winning Metro 6R4 that was discovered languishing in a scrapyard.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz are off to Wolverhampton to take on a 1980 Jaguar XJ-S V12, a great British Grand Tourer that has sadly fallen into neglect.

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Lexi hopes to bond with Sam and Jackson. Bobby and Milly try to mend Johnnos broken heart. And puppy Cookie may be too much for dancers Ash and Mike.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: After recovering from leukaemia, Toby is driven to build a complex home around an old oak tree in Suffolk. But can he balance high architecture and health with a comfy family place?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns, as he follows more ambitious self-builds. Zahid and Ferzana transform an old coastguard station set on exposed East Yorkshire headland into a three-storey glass box.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Abby hit her head after falling from the steps of a high diving board. The medical team need to scan her head and neck to check for serious injuries. Meanwhile, the hospital security team deals with over 50 incidents every month in AandE, ranging from verbal abuse to assault. Anne and Holton are both working the night shift, dealing with a variety of challenging patients in the hospital.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: This episode takes a moving - and sometimes light-hearted - look at when boys become men, and passing the baton between the generations. Twenty-six-year-old Nicholas has been punched in the face in a random attack. Meanwhile, Tyrell has damaged his big toe playing football, and Ho has brought his 78-year-old grandma Amoui to Kings after finding her collapsed in her flat.

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man has been making repeated calls claiming to be having a heart attack. He lives near the hospital and in the past has used an ambulance as a taxi to get him home. Paramedics also treat a woman who is in agony with a slipped disc.

programme.desc: Ray Bradshaw tells a loveable tale of growing up with deaf parents, laced with pranks and playful, witty anecdotes of family life in his signature cheeky Glaswegian humour.

programme.title: Anna Mann: How We Stop the Fascists
programme.desc: Anna Mann, aka greatest actress of her generation, and Colin Hoults alter-ego, takes the audience on a whirlwind journey, to understand where fascism can spring from.

programme.title: Alistair Barrie - Alistaircratic
programme.desc: Alistair brings his latest hour of topical musings, but this was written in March and you can never be too sure whats going to happen next these days.

programme.title: Ian Smith: Half Life
programme.desc: Narrative stand-up show from comedian Ian Smith, who doesnt do half measures - theres love, theres stress, theres a disabled pigeon... and theres a holiday in Chernobyl.

programme.title: Shelf: Hair
programme.desc: Shelf are a musical comedy double act. Sort of. One of them is tone deaf, so technically theyre a musical single act plus guest. They take to the stage for a night of musical stand-up.

programme.title: Esther Manito: Crusade
programme.desc: Wife, Mother, Arab, British, Muslim: Esther is on a mission to show you what life is like when you cant meet the expectations of your identity. From battling ridiculous comments online to facing head-on confrontations in person, Esther asks what happens if you decide to reject the role of the Crusader.

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Defined
programme.desc: Juliette Burton goes on her journey of self discovery (and mental health recovery) in asking: why cant we be everything at once?

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: A moving, inspirational hour of stigma-busting gags, multimedia projection, hidden camera filming and animation.

programme.desc: Peppa and his friends are at school and have a special guest, Mr. Potatoes. Mr. Potatoes is there to give them a fruit and vegetable test.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig hangs his clean football shirt out to dry, but Peppa, George and Mummy splash it with mud. They put it in the wash, but Peppa puts her red dress in too - which turns Daddys shirt pink.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George go on a boat trip with Grandpa Pig, but they must send Polly Parrot for help when their boat runs aground. Polly fetches Granny Pig and Granddad Dog to rescue them.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Delphine the Donkey comes to stay. Peppa takes Delphine to playgroup, where they teach her the `Bing Bong Song.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppas family get caught in a very long traffic jam whilst on the way to have lunch with Granny and Grandpa Pig.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When Mummy Pig helps out at Miss Rabbits fire station, she is called upon to douse the flames at Daddy Pigs out-of-control barbecue.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig come for a dinner party with Mummy and Daddy Pig. Peppa and George are too tired to go to bed, until they are told a story.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa brings home a teddy from playgroup to look after, but she is worried that he will not have a very exciting time.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is Danny Dogs birthday, and with the help of Grandad Dog and Grandpa Pig the children take part in a pirate treasure hunt.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle takes Peppa and her friends on a train ride where they must tick off what they see on their activity sheets.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny Pig shows Peppa and George her poultry. Elsewhere, something has been eating Grandpa Pigs lettuces.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: At Peppas playgroup, Madame Gazelle wants everyone to show the class their special talent.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppas family go to the museums moon exhibition with Georges friend, Edmond Elephant.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppas tooth falls out and she puts it under her pillow. Theres a shiny coin under her pillow when she wakes up. How did that happen?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Grandpa Pig takes Peppa and George to the playground, but does not understand the childrens rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Goldie the fish does not look happy and is not eating her food. Peppa, George, and Mummy Pig take her on a bus trip to visit Dr Hamster the vet.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and the family go to the funfair, but Mummy Pig is angry when people at the fair tell her that she will not be able to win prizes in the competitions.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The youngsters visit the zoo where they are shown around by zookeepers Mr Lion, Mrs Crocodile and Mr Giraffe.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbits Rescue Service has saved many people from tricky situations, but what happens when Miss Rabbit needs rescuing herself.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is determined to find the perfect name for the baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa receives an invite to Candy Cats birthday party, but a double-decker party bus arrives; the bus goes to the playground and even the beach, hosting the best party ever.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emilys house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Animated series about a lovable little pig.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and Georges toy basket is full and their beds are covered in toys, so Mummy Pig decides it is time to order a new toy cupboard. The cupboard arrives in the post but it needs to be assembled - and Mummy and Daddy Pig are not very good at DIY. Luckily help is at hand, as Mr Zebra the Postman loves assembling flat pack furniture.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pigs book is finished, and its time to show it off to Peppa and her playgroup friends. Unfortunately, Peppa and George lose some of the book when they are playing, and Mummy Pig has to think quickly to fix it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When George loses the metal wind-up key for his toy robot in the long grass, Grandpa Pig cannot find his metal detector to help George track it down.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The Playgroup play many games using a parachute.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: In the game group, Peppa and his friends make their own instruments from old wrappers.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Its Wendy Wolfs birthday and she has invited Peppa and all her friends to her party! There are bubbles and balloons to play with - and Wendy even teaches them how to howl like a wolf!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family hire a canoe from Miss Rabbit. They imagine they are in a jungle and then unexpectedly bump into Mrs Crocodile. Later on, Mr Lion invites them to have a picnic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Lotte Llama joins the playgroup, and teaches the others how to speak German.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig and Peppa are at Granny Pigs house. They find Mummys old patchwork quilt that Granny made for her as a baby. Peppa decides shed like to make one for the baby!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is so excited to meet her new baby brother or sister, but they are taking forever to arrive until suddenly Mummy Pig announces the baby is coming, now!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a beautiful sunny day, Grandpa takes Peppa and George to spend the day sailing. After an accident, the boat ends up sinking, and now they need rescuing with a hovercraft.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When the Police visit the school, officers Panda and Squirrel teach Peppa and her friends all about bicycle safety.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends play some very active games in the garden and together they learn to hop, skip and jump.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle teaches the children how to play the recorder. They practise a lot at home, then put on a performance for all the parents to enjoy.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George and Daddy Pig visit the charity shop to sell things they dont need, and things they may need.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The nurse is popping around, and she decides that todays visit should be all about how to hold a baby. The nurse shows Peppa a special way for a small person to safely hold a baby.

programme.desc: Animated series about a lovable little pig.

programme.desc: Peppa and family go on a walk through the forest where they meet all of their favourite fairytale characters. They visit the home of bears who like porridge and meet a hungry Mr. Wolf.

programme.desc: The Carnival has come to town and its a chance to see all sorts of exciting things! There is Mr. Bulls Jazz Band, Miss Rabbit as the Carnival Queen and even a very special appearance from Mr. Potato.

programme.desc: Rebecca and Richard Rabbit are playing a marble run game at Peppas house. But when Daddy Pig joins in and improvises tunnels and bridges, the marble rolls out of the bedroom, causing chaos and nearly knocking Goldie out of her bowl.

programme.desc: Peppa and family are watching TV... until it breaks! While Grampy Rabbit fixes it, the family get a new TV which can do everything... but it doesnt beat their old TV.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppas family is staying at Cousin Chloes house. Its been a long day and everyone is tired and sleepy, but baby Alexander keeps everyone awake with his crying.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Eek! Theres a spider in the house. Mummy Pig tells Daddy Pig to get rid of it. Mr Skinny-Legs is set free in the garden, where he can spin all the webs he likes.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Playgroup is having a pet competition and everyone has brought their pets. Dr Hamster is the judge, but it is so hard to choose the best pet.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.sub-title: Grandpa Pigs Train to the Rescue
programme.desc: Oh dear! Miss Rabbits train has broken down and all her passengers need to get to work. So Grandpa Pig lends Miss Rabbit his little train, Gertrude.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: George pays a visit to Mr Foxs Shop after his favourite toy, Mr Dinosaur, breaks. With all the additional features, surely Dino Roar is a good replacement?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family have been on a trip to a mountain beauty spot. When Daddy Pig loses the car keys down a drain, Mr Bull offers to find them with his digger.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: To celebrate Freddy Foxs birthday, the children go on a trip to a Dinosaur Park. With Grampy Rabbit as their leader, they follow dinosaur footprints to find Freddys birthday treat.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Dog and Danny are very excited! Captain Daddy Dog is home from the sea, and hes bearing gifts! What can they be?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George meet Kylie Kangaroo and her brother Joey. Peppa shows Kylie how to jump up and down in muddy puddles, but who can jump the highest?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When Peppa goes for a sleepover at Zoe Zebras house with Suzy Sheep, Rebecca Rabbit, Candy Cat and Emily Elephant, the girls are too excited to go to sleep.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Its Daddy Pigs birthday, but he has to go to work, so while hes away, Mummy Pig, Peppa and George prepare a special birthday surprise.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy and Daddy Pig let Peppa and George put their coats on over their pyjamas and go outside to look at the stars. With the help of a telescope, Daddy Pig teaches Peppa and George how to find the North Star.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are playing in the garden with their bouncy ball when Suzy Sheep turns up with two tennis rackets. She uses the bouncy ball to play a game of tennis with Peppa, making George feel left out. Will the job of ball boy cheer him up?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When a power cut plunges their house into darkness, Peppa and George have fun looking for a torch and thinking of games to play in the dark.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family borrow Grandpa Pigs boat and head out for a day on the river. All is going well until the boat gets stuck in some long reeds - Daddy Pig to the rescue.

programme.desc: Peppa and her friends go on a camping trip with their teacher, Madame Gazelle. After pitching their tents and collecting sticks, they all sing a song around the campfire.

programme.desc: Peppa and Suzy Sheep are best friends, but one day they have a quarrel over a game of cards, and decide they are not best friends anymore.

programme.desc: Peppa and George are helping Granny and Grandpa Pig tidy their attic, but they have trouble deciding what to throw out, because Granny and Grandpa want to keep everything.

programme.desc: Peppa and her family follow a nature trail in the countryside, discovering footprints left by little birds and ants.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George go to Rebecca Rabbits house where, instead of stairs and corridors, there are tunnels.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family visit a museum, and Peppa likes the Kings and Queens room, whereas Georges favourite is the dinosaur room.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Fed up with seeing how dependent The Chipmunks have become on technology, Dave takes them camping for a weekend, without electricity, cell service or the Internet.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany is humiliated by Jeanettes lack of style. That is, until Jeanette is proclaimed a fashion trendsetter by Brittanys style icon!

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin catches Simon taking a personality test to discover his soul-mate, he decides to do the same and prove just how silly the test really is.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin inadvertently destroys Theodores bicycle, he enters the boys in a roundup of shopping carts where the first prize is a new bike.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin sees a sparkling diamond buried in his neighbours rose garden, hes sure shes responsible for the recent diamond heist, so Alvin sets about proving it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave is thrilled when he thinks hes nominated for a Grammy, but its actually an award for aging musicians. Alvin and Kevin pretend to be sick to miss school and sneak off to a convention.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: In an effort to be more productive, Simon invents a device that can override the need to sleep; Alvin gets a taste of his own medicine when he zaps Dave with a youth ray, turning him into an irresponsible teen.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodores new talking teddy bear drives Alvin and Simon crazy. Alvin is not thrilled about going back to school, until he sees the new principal.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave is working on an important new song for the music festival, but the Chipmunks accidentally ruin it. Can they fix it in time for the big performance?

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simon invents a robot with emotions and an ability to learn which backfires when hes exposed to the internet.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave finds himself in school with Alvin after he discovers that he never graduated from high school; Simons new friend decides to ditch him to spend time with Alvin.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin encourages Simon to write an email, venting about his professor. When the email accidentally gets sent, they must stop the teacher from reading it. When Simon notices that Theodore has a hard time saying no, he creates the No-Buddy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gives Dave an ugly handmade vest for his birthday and insists he wear it to an upcoming business meeting. When Theodore finds a winning lottery ticket, everyone has their own ideas on how the money should be spent.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Simon Says; Sing Like a Canary
programme.desc: When Alvin accuses Simon of being too safe, Simon agrees to do whatever crazy thing Alvin wants him to do. A cute singing bird replaces Alvin in the band after Alvin makes too many demands as a performer.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After getting detention again, Alvins convinced Dave will drive him to the desert and abandon him there; Alvin and Theodore play a seemingly innocent game, which leads to unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is passed over when Alvin and Eleanor train as lifeguards; Miss Croners cherries are lost.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Desperate to avenge last years humiliating loss, the chipmunks argue over who is best suited to lead them to victory; Alvin grows tired of seeing his celebrity status overshadowed by reality TV stars, so he decides to become one himself.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Were the Chipmunks; Save the Dance
programme.desc: When a new boy band, The Hipmunks, tries to steal the spotlight, the Chipmunks are forced to compete in a battle of the bands. The Principal gives Alvin and Derek an ultimatum, if they fight, the school dance will be cancelled.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys get stuck with Miss Miller as a baby sitter, they each take a crack at finding a replacement. Alvin volunteers to caddy a charity golf tournament and surprisingly gets paired up with Miss Smith.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The Chipmunks and Chipettes compete in a dodgeball tournament but no-one expects Theo to be their secret weapon. The Chipmunks find a shack that magically fills with toys and games, but is it magic or something more sinister.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: The Curse Of Macbeth / Zeela The Great
programme.desc: The Chipmunks dont want to perform in the school play, after learning of its curse; Simons robot Geizmo becomes jealous of Jeanettes robot Zeela, until a sudden tragedy reveals Geizmos true heart.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodores new talking teddy bear drives Alvin and Simon crazy. Alvin is not thrilled about going back to school, until he sees the new principal.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: I Will Survive; Alvins Got a Brand New Bag
programme.desc: When Alvin gets hurt, Simon agrees to face off against Derek on a survival reality show; Alvin accidentally ruins Brittanys favourite bag and must come up with a way to replace it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin borrows Simons invisibility suit, but gets trapped when the zipper gets stuck. After Theodore makes a wish, the Chipmunks try to learn what it was so they can make it come true.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Theodores teddy bear, Talking Teddy, gets bear-napped, the boys work with Officer Dangus to get him back. When Alvin starts stealing the morning paper to sell at school, Dave blames the paperboy and refuses to pay up.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore helps Simon to try and get past his perfectionism in order to complete an art assignment for art class. Simon has a crush on the babysitter and secretly prepares a romantic dinner while Theodore keeps Alvin distracted.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simons interactive video game is about to take off, until Jeanette reprograms the games villain; when Alvin dreams of joining a private club, he volunteers Dave to play squash, even though Dave doesnt play.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin catches Simon taking a test to discover his soul mate. Simon and Alvin decide to rebel and launch their own campaigns for class president.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin helps the mathletes stand up to the school bullies, but in the process they become bullies themselves; Alvin befriends a new student, but Theodore learns something surprising about him.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin is convinced that Theodore has cursed him to never lie again and cant help himself from blabbing the truth.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When one of Simons inventions malfunctions, Jeanette and Alvin switch places and now each must live as the other.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Theos Big Night Out; The Bodyguard
programme.desc: Theodore tries to overcome his fear of sleepovers by spending the night at Kevins house without Alvin or Simon. Plus, when Alvin sees every celebrity with their own personal bodyguard, he decides to train Theodore for the job.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Animal House Party; Dr. Zap vs. Electroboy
programme.desc: When Derek overhears that Alvin and his brothers are alone for the weekend, he plans a wild party at their house. Theodore is hit by lightning and believes hes like his comic book hero, until hes told hes actually a villain.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Simon falls ill, Alvin takes his place on a camping trip only to discover its actually an archaeological dig; the Chipmunks dont know what to do when they suspect Dave is lying about the various awards hes won.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin believes his mojo was in a cap he regrets giving to a fan. During a visit to the zoo, Alvin is mistaken for a Tasmanian devil.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simon creates a Super Hero costume for a convention hes attending. But when Alvin wants to join, the two fight over who the real hero is. When Alvins bicycle is stolen, he joins forces with Officer Dangus to solve the case.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is stuck taking care of Miss Croners cats during a week that Dave wants the boys to focus only on school. The Chipettes challenge the Chipmunks to go three hours without their favourite things.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittanys fear of bees begins to turn into admiration when shes shrunk down to their size and has to experience life as a bee.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Simon invents a robot with emotions and an ability to learn which backfires when hes exposed to the internet.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: You Troll; Life of the Party
programme.desc: The Chipmunks try to keep Dave from getting on the Internet after a performance he posts gets negative comments. The Chipmunks along with Kevin and Cheesy decide to crash BoCarters birthday yacht party after not being invited.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: A single father experiences the trials and tribulations of raising a group of young chipmunks who are also famous rock stars touring the world with their best friends, the Chipettes.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Dave finds himself in school with Alvin after he discovers that he never graduated from high school; Simons new friend decides to ditch him to spend time with Alvin.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvin encourages Simon to write an email, venting about his professor. When the email accidentally gets sent, they must stop the teacher from reading it. When Simon notices that Theodore has a hard time saying no, he creates the No-Buddy.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gives Dave an ugly handmade vest for his birthday and insists he wear it to an upcoming business meeting. When Theodore finds a winning lottery ticket, everyone has their own ideas on how the money should be spent.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Simon Says; Sing Like a Canary
programme.desc: When Alvin accuses Simon of being too safe, Simon agrees to do whatever crazy thing Alvin wants him to do. A cute singing bird replaces Alvin in the band after Alvin makes too many demands as a performer.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After getting detention again, Alvins convinced Dave will drive him to the desert and abandon him there; Alvin and Theodore play a seemingly innocent game, which leads to unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is passed over when Alvin and Eleanor train as lifeguards; Miss Croners cherries are lost.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Desperate to avenge last years humiliating loss, the chipmunks argue over who is best suited to lead them to victory; Alvin grows tired of seeing his celebrity status overshadowed by reality TV stars, so he decides to become one himself.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When the boys get tired of taking orders from Dave, they decide to build a tree house in the backyard and move out. When an invention of Simons accidentally gets knocked over it magnetizes Alvin and Simon making them inseparable.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Alvin accuses Dave of yelling at him too much, a therapist moves in to help Dave control his anger. When the school digs up an old high school time capsule, Miss Smith and Dave work together to keep an old secret.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Alvins too short to ride a new amusement park ride; while on a ski trip, Alvin convinces Theodore theres a Yeti nearby.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Stuck inside the house due to a storm, Simon invents a virtual reality game that quickly gets out of hand; Cheesy enlists Theodore and Alvin to help steal desserts from the school kitchen using secret underground tunnels.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: I Will Survive; Alvins Got a Brand New Bag
programme.desc: When Alvin gets hurt, Simon agrees to face off against Derek on a survival reality show; Alvin accidentally ruins Brittanys favourite bag and must come up with a way to replace it.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: After Brittany and the girls release a video that goes viral, Alvin, Simon, and Theodore try to top it. Simon falls ill and asks Alvin and Theodore not to go see a new movie without him, but Alvin just cannot resist.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: The town is under attack when Simon creates a drone that can not only deliver pizzas but has the ability to learn. Alvin worries the munks are responsible for making Dave boring when they find out he used to be a rock star.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When an accident forces Simon to wear his old glasses, he becomes the target of the school bully. So Simon builds a homemade robot bodyguard!

programme.title: The Black Keys - Live at Main Square Festival
programme.desc: Main Square Festival is a music festival that takes place in Arras, France, lasting three days.

programme.title: Pet Shop Boys: Live at Roskilde 2009
programme.desc: Filmed in Roskilde, Denmark, this Pet Shop Boys concert features hits: "Always On My Mind," "West End Girls," and more.

programme.title: Lana Del Rey - Live in London 2012
programme.desc: Born to Die is the second album by singer-songwriter Lana Del Rey, and the first to be officially released on January 27, 2012 and that would lead to global success; In countries such as the United Kingdom the album was the best selling of that year.

programme.title: Paul Simon: Live in New York City
programme.desc: Paul Simon performs some of his most beloved and classic songs, as well as selections from his critically acclaimed release, "So Beautiful or So What," in a 2011 concert.

programme.title: Carrie Underwood - Live in London 2015
programme.desc: Watch Carrie Underwood performing live at the Apple Music Festival. What a unique concert experience, not only for the fans at the Roundhouse, but also for fans across the globe!

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix - Live at Woodstock
programme.desc: Jimi Hendrixs explosive interpretation of The Star-Spangled Banner from the Woodstock Music Festival remains one of the most definitive moments in rock history.

programme.title: Foo Fighters - Austin City Limits
programme.desc: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

programme.title: Willie Nelson: Live at iTunes Festival
programme.desc: American country singer and guitarist Willie Nelson performs his top tracks at the 2014 iTunes Festival.

programme.title: James Taylor - Austin City Limits
programme.desc: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas; the festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

programme.title: Soundgarden - Live in London 2014
programme.desc: Soundgarden, an American rock band, performs at the 2014 iTunes Festival.

programme.title: Green Day: Live at Fox Theater
programme.desc: Green Day performs from "21st Century Breakdown", at the Fox Theater in the bands hometown, Oakland, California.

programme.title: Alice in Chains - Unplugged
programme.desc: In their first live concert in over 2 years (and possibly their last concert ever), Alternative/metal group Alice in Chains performs acoustically at MTV Unplugged. Includes acoustic versions of their hits.

programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives. Born without a trace, they have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. programme.title: The Cinema List: Jurassic
programme.desc: Welcome to a Cinema List 65 million years in the making, going through the very best moments from the Jurassic franchise - from the 1993 original to `Jurassic World: Dominion.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A widowed farmer, with a brave but brutal military past, decides not to join up when the British arrive in 1776. However, when his son enlists and is later captured by the enemy, the former soldier must abandon his new-found pacifist principles in order to rescue his oldest child, and forms a regiment of Carolina patriots, whose guerrilla tactics prove pivotal to the US war effort.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: Adventure sequel. Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones, on the trail of fortune and glory in Old Shanghai, is ricocheted into a dangerous adventure in India. With his faithful companion Short Round and nightclub singer Willie Scott, Indie goes in search of the magical Sankara stone, and uncovers an ancient evil which threatens all who come into contact with it.

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire
programme.desc: Greek Gen. Themistocles leads the charge to fight invading Persian forces led by the god Xerxes -- once a mortal -- and Artemisia.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of the Jurassic World theme park, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing return to the island of Isla Nublar to save the remaining dinosaurs from a volcano thats about to erupt. They soon encounter terrifying new breeds of gigantic dinosaurs, while uncovering a conspiracy that threatens the entire planet.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: Snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, young Furiosa gets caught in the crossfire of two tyrannical warlords. As the tyrants fight for dominance, Furiosa soon finds herself in a nonstop battle to make her way home.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6
programme.desc: Since Dom and Brians heist in Rio they must live as fugitives. Meanwhile, Agent Hobbs has been tracking a gang of lethally skilled mercenary drivers. Hobbs asks Dom and his crew for help in exchange for full pardons for everyone.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Geeky highschooler Sam Witwicky buys his first-ever car, hoping it will improve his chances with Mikaela. Imagine his surprise when his beat-down Camaro transforms into an alien robot called `Bumblebee and asks for his assistance in a deadly war.

programme.title: Bad Boys: Ride or Die
programme.desc: When their late police captain gets linked to drug cartels, wisecracking Miami cops Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett embark on a dangerous mission to clear his name.

programme.title: Batman Forever
programme.desc: DA Harvey Dent and vengeful scientist The Riddler plan to take down Batman, as Dent wrongly believes that it is Batmans fault he was disfigured, and the scientist is angry with Bruce Wayne for rejecting his experiments. The Gotham City superhero is going to need his new partner, boy wonder Robin, but will he be able to cope with the distraction of sexy doctor Chase Meridian?

programme.title: Fast and Furious 5
programme.desc: In Rio de Janeiro, ex-con Dom Torretto and ex-cop Brian OConner join forces against a corrupt businessman who wants them both dead.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 5
programme.desc: In Rio de Janeiro, ex-con Dom Torretto and ex-cop Brian OConner join forces against a corrupt businessman who wants them both dead.

programme.desc: Reverse-Flash manipulates the Speed Force to put The Flash into a time loop, which forces him to re-live the same day over and over again. The Flash must find a way to restore time and finally apprehend his worst enemy before all is lost.

programme.desc: Batman and Robin have their hands full when Penguin, The Joker, Riddler and Catwoman join forces.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go! and DC Super Hero Girls: Mayhem in the Multiverse

programme.title: Fox and Hare Save the Forest
programme.desc: One day, Fox and Hare discover that Owl has disappeared. They go looking for him in the forest with all their friends and discover a big lake that wasnt there before. It is the beginning of an exciting and funny adventure.

programme.title: Dog Man
programme.desc: A life-saving surgery fuses together a police officer and his faithful police dog -- and Dog Man is born. Learning to embrace his new identity, Dog Man must stop feline supervillain Petey the Cat from cloning himself and going on a crime spree.

programme.title: Rock Dog 2: Rock Around the Park
programme.desc: Canine rocker Bodi and his band discover the price of fame when they go on tour with pop sensation Lil Foxy.

programme.title: Riverdance
programme.desc: An Irish boy and a Spanish girl journey into the mythical world of the Megaloceros Giganteus who teach them to appreciate Riverdance as a celebration of life.

programme.desc: The village is being overrun by rabbits and Wallace has a plan to alter their minds so that they will leave the vegetable allotments alone. However, his plan goes awry and the rabbit problem escalates when the villagers discover a monstrous `were-rabbit destroying everything in its sights. Will Wallace and Gromit be able to solve the conundrum before the creature gets shot?

programme.title: Paws of Fury: The Legend of Hank
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck hound finds himself in a town full of cats who need a hero to defend them from a ruthless villains evil plot. With help from a reluctant trainer, the underdog must assume the role of fearsome samurai and save the day.

programme.title: Trolls
programme.desc: Poppy, the optimistic leader of the trolls, and Branch, her polar opposite, embark on an adventure that takes them far beyond the only world theyve ever known.

programme.title: Despicable Me 4
programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., whos intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: The Amazing Maurice
programme.desc: Maurice is a streetwise ginger cat who comes up with a money-making scam by befriending a group of self-taught talking rats. When Maurice and the rodents meet a bookworm called Malicia, their little con soon goes down the drain.

programme.title: Batman: Return of the Caped Crusaders
programme.desc: Batman and Robin have their hands full when Penguin, The Joker, Riddler and Catwoman join forces.

programme.title: Justice League Vs. Bizarro League

programme.title: Teen Titans Go! and DC Super Hero Girls: Mayhem in the Multiverse

programme.title: Ted 2
programme.desc: Teddy bear Ted seeks legal help from a young lawyer and a legendary, civil-rights attorney when the law declares him to be property and not a person.

programme.title: Mean Girls
programme.desc: After 15 years in African bush country, Cady knows all about herd instincts, but nothing has prepared her for high school. She joins a popular but cruelly manipulative crowd of girls known as the `Plastics and secretly mocks them, but the fun stops when one betrays her and steals the boy she fancies.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers
programme.desc: The story of a pair of blues musicians who try to raise the money to save an orphanage by reuniting their old band - nearly writing off Chicago in the process.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: American Pie: Reunion
programme.desc: Outrageous antics ensue when Jim, Michelle, Stifler and the gang get back together for their 13-year high school reunion.

programme.title: Role Models
programme.desc: Danny is sour after driving away his girlfriend, and Wheeler is into anything wearing a skirt. The two get into a fight with a trucker and choose community service over a jail term.

programme.title: Mean Girls
programme.desc: After 15 years in African bush country, Cady knows all about herd instincts, but nothing has prepared her for high school. She joins a popular but cruelly manipulative crowd of girls known as the `Plastics and secretly mocks them, but the fun stops when one betrays her and steals the boy she fancies.

programme.title: Blades of Glory
programme.desc: Chazz Michael Michaels and Jimmy MacElroy are the two titans of skating. At the Winter Olympics, the fierce rivals find themselves forced to share the winners podium when they both score gold. The shower of glory doesnt last for long, as the pair descend into fisticuffs and set the mascot on fire. Disgraced and stripped of their medals, they can get back on ice, but only if they dance with each other as a pair.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop III
programme.desc: Axel Foley and his Beverly Hills buddies investigate criminal activities at a Southern California amusement park.

programme.title: Just Go With It
programme.desc: His heart recently broken, plastic surgeon Danny Maccabee (Adam Sandler) pretends to be married so he can enjoy future dates with no strings attached. His web of lies works, but when he meets Palmer (Brooklyn Decker) -- the gal of his dreams -- she resists involvement. Instead of coming clean, Danny enlists Katherine (Jennifer Aniston), his assistant, to pose as his soon-to-be-ex-wife. Instead of solving Dannys problems, the lies create more trouble.

programme.title: Daddys Home 2
programme.desc: Brad and Dustys respective fathers show up to turn Christmastime upside down.

programme.title: The School of Rock
programme.desc: After guitarist Dewey is kicked out of his own band, he pretends to be a teacher. When he hears the kids play, he realises they could be his support group in the Battle of the Bands, and hell finally get to be the rock star he knows hes destined to be.

programme.title: The Man With Two Brains
programme.desc: Zany horror spoof about a brilliant brain surgeon who falls in love with a female brain stored in a jar. The brain is part of an impressive collection held by a colleague in Austria and, upon learning of his wifes infidelity, the surgeon allows himself to be charmed. Communicating telepathically with one of the specimens in the laboratory, he falls in love - but needs a body to put it in.

programme.title: The Natural
programme.desc: The almost mythical ability of a middle-aged baseball player rockets a major-league team toward the 1939 pennant.

programme.title: The Natural

programme.title: Turtles All the Way Down
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old struggling with obsessive-compulsive disorder reconnects with her childhood crush, she faces the potential of finding love and happiness despite her mental condition.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Drift
programme.desc: Young Liberian refugee Jacqueline barely escapes her war-torn country to settle on a Greek island. Her daily struggle for survival keeps terrible memories at bay, and she becomes close to an American tour guide.

programme.title: The Shamrock Spitfire
programme.desc: The youngest wing commander in the history of the Royal Air Force, 21-year-old Brendan Spitfire Paddy Finucane became one of the most celebrated fighter aces of the Second World War.
