programme.desc: Hard-drinking country music star Jackson Maine discovers, and falls in love with, a struggling but talented singer named Ally. As her career quickly takes off, Jackson starts to realize that his best days may be behind him.

programme.title: The Good Half
programme.desc: A man returns home to Cleveland for his mothers funeral, where he must confront his grief, reconnect with estranged family members, and forge new relationships.

programme.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
programme.desc: Struggling with his dual identity, failed comedian Arthur Fleck stumbles upon true love while incarcerated at Arkham State Hospital. Awaiting trial for the murder of five people, Fleck soon discovers the music thats always been inside him.

programme.title: Drift
programme.desc: Young Liberian refugee Jacqueline barely escapes her war-torn country to settle on a Greek island. Her daily struggle for survival keeps terrible memories at bay, and she becomes close to an American tour guide.

programme.title: Elizabeth: The Golden Age
programme.desc: Elizabeth might have her royal rival Mary Queen of Scots in custody, but a far greater threat lies to the east: the Spanish Armada. Despite her reign of 30 years, Elizabeths hold on the English throne remains uncertain. Her kingdom lies in the shadow of mighty Spain, Philip IIs vast army and the Inquisition, but Elizabeth is undaunted.

programme.title: About Time
programme.desc: After discovering that he can travel through time, a young man uses his ability to win the woman of his dreams and save his friend from professional disaster.

programme.title: Jackie
programme.desc: The days leading up to the tragedy of President John F. Kennedys assassination and its immediate aftermath are chronicled as they were experienced by the First Lady, wife of the charismatic president and mother of two small children.

programme.title: The Brutalist
programme.desc: Escaping postwar Europe, a visionary architect comes to America to rebuild his life, his career, and his marriage. On his own in a strange new country, he settles in Pennsylvania, where a wealthy and prominent industrialist recognises his talent.

programme.desc: Washed-up stuntman Luo seeks help from his estranged daughter and her lawyer boyfriend when his beloved stunt horse may be auctioned off to pay his debts. Luo and Red Hare become a media sensation when their fight with debt collectors goes viral.

programme.title: Spy Kids
programme.desc: Two siblings set out on a mission to rescue their ex-spy parents. Summoned back to work from their uneventful lives, the adults run into trouble while on duty. When the children are called upon to help them, they find out about their parents former identities while at the same time developing a greater appreciation for each other.

programme.title: Tom and Jerry: Cowboy Up!
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry are in the Wild West where they help save a ranch from the hands of a greedy villain. The rivals team up to help a cowgirl and her brother save their homestead from a greedy land-grabber.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part II
programme.desc: Old rivalries and new adversaries await Daniel and Miyagi as they travel to Okinawa to visit Miyagis ailing father.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part III
programme.desc: A boy trained in the martial arts is alienated from his mentor just when he needs to prepare for a showdown with his arch-enemy. Instead, he turns to a Vietnam vet who teaches him how to fight dirty - a strategy the youth comes to realise is a mistake.

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa
programme.desc: The sequel to `Madagascar, in which New York Zoo animals, Alex the Lion, Marty the Zebra, Melman the Giraffe and Gloria the Hippo, still stranded on Madagascar, start to leave the island. All of a sudden, they land in the wilderness of Africa, where Alex meets the rest of his family, but has trouble communicating with them after spending so much time at the Central Park Zoo.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie
programme.desc: Three emojis embark on an epic adventure through a smartphone to save their world from being deleted.

programme.title: Bee Movie
programme.desc: Barry B Benson is an everybee with a dream. He escapes the hive and makes contact with a human, the florist Vanessa Bloome. Enraged to discover that humans have been stealing their honey goodness, Barry sets out on a crusade that will change the ecosystem forever. Will it be for the better? A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: Slapstick farce with the incompetent cops as they set about saving their beloved academy from closure. Cutbacks mean that only one of the two police training schools can stay open, so Commandant Lassard appeals to the motley crew of graduates to come and train new recruits. However, the rival academy intends to sabotage their plans by cutting their training time down to a minimum.

programme.title: Terms of Endearment

programme.title: Little Women
programme.desc: Marmee is the head of the March household in the absence of her husband, whos away fighting in the US Civil War. Her daughters range from the headstrong Jo and sweet Beth, to domesticated Meg and wilful Amy. The film follows the trials and tribulations of the girls, from early poverty and tragedy to Jos later efforts to secure both a husband and a successful writing career, and Amys relationship with an old family friend.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.title: The Krays

The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

programme.title: Cool Hand Luke
programme.desc: A gutsy prisoner earns the admiration of inmates and the wrath of guards by constant defiance and bids for freedom.

programme.desc: A middle-class snob on the eve of retirement takes refuge with her bohemian sister after she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend.

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet
programme.desc: Hoping to stay in the country, a gay man proposes a green card marriage to a female friend in exchange for paying for her IVF treatment. However, things get complicated when his grandmother surprises them with an extravagant Korean wedding banquet.

programme.title: The Taming of the Shrew
programme.desc: Petruchio pursues the fiery Katharina in Padua, Italy, during the 1500s in Shakespeares battle of the sexes. Petruchio tries to woo Katharina so that his friend can marry her sister.

programme.title: Forces of Nature
programme.desc: A writer is trying to fly from New York to Savannah, Georgia, where he is due to be married in two days time. However, the plane crashes during take-off and the writer inadvertently saves the life of one of his fellow passengers, a free-spirited woman. She then accompanies him as he tries to continue his journey by rental car, train and bus - but he is beset by one disaster after another.

programme.title: Mamma Mia!
programme.desc: Set on a Greek island, a young woman who is about to be married suspects that any one of three men could be her father. She invites them all to her wedding without telling her mother. Movie adaptation of the musical, set to Abbas greatest hits.

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
programme.desc: In 1979 Donna falls in love with Sam while embarking on a series of adventures throughout Europe. In the present day, Donnas pregnant daughter reunites with her mothers old friends and boyfriends on the Greek island of Kalokairi.

programme.title: The Holiday
programme.desc: Dumped and depressed, English rose Iris agrees to swap homes with similarly unlucky in love Californian Amanda for a much-needed break. Iris finds herself in a palatial Hollywood mansion while Amanda navigates the lanes of a picture-perfect English village. Soon enough, both lovelorn ladies bump into local lads perfect for a romantic pick-me-up.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality
programme.desc: An uncouth FBI agent goes undercover in the Miss United States beauty pageant as a contestant to flush out a terrorist who plans to bomb the event. The hard-nosed cop, who prides herself on her toughness, must discard her trainers and feminise her behaviour if she is to avoid blowing her cover.

programme.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
programme.desc: Andie Anderson writes an advice column for a fashionable magazine and agrees to demonstrate how easy it is to attract then alienate a man in just ten days. She chooses Benjamin Barry, little knowing hes an advertising executive whose job rests on proving he understands women so well that he can make one fall for him in two weeks.

programme.title: The 40-Year-Old Virgin
programme.desc: Electronics store stock boy Andy Stitzer is horrified when he is found out as a virgin by his workmates - just when he thought he was becoming one of the gang. David, Jay and Cal surprise him by making it their mission to get him a girl and help him lose his virginity.

programme.title: Love Actually
programme.desc: Weaving in and out of the love lives of eight loosely inter-related couples. The bachelor Prime Minister falls for his personal assistant, his brother-in-law is flirting with one of his colleagues, a writer heads off to France and pursues an affair with a maid, and an 11-year-old boy asks his father for advice on matters of the heart.

programme.title: Singin in the Rain
programme.desc: When the transition is being made from silent films to `talkies, everyone has trouble adapting. Don and Lina have been cast repeatedly as a romantic couple, but when their latest film is remade into a musical, only Don has the voice for the new singing part. After a lot of practise with a diction coach, Lina still sounds terrible, and Kathy, a bright young aspiring actress, is hired to record over her voice.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the 100th episode of the popular US sitcom, Leonard decides to ask Penny out on a date, and they both agree to give their relationship another chance. Later they agree to continue seeing each other secretly, on the condition that they act like friends when everyone else is around.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: When Sheldon and Kripke argue over who should get Professor Rothmans vacant corner office, Leonard, Raj and Howard devise a contest to settle the issue with the office as the winning prize.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldons well-organised world is turned upside down when he learns that the barber who has cut his hair for years has passed away. As his orderly routines descend into chaos, he is forced to act like a hippy in order to match his shaggy new hairstyle. Meanwhile, Howard is sent to Houston for the gruelling NASA training programme and shares horrific stories about his experiences with Bernadette via Skype.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy gets upset with Sheldon when he breaks his promise to accompany her to her aunts birthday party and instead opts to join the guys for a `Star Wars video game marathon lasting the entire weekend. Later, Penny convinces Amy that she should confront Sheldon about the matter and demand an apology.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Desperate to find a `plus one to take to a wedding, Raj finally gives up and asks his parents to set him up with a date. Meanwhile, Penny buys Leonard and Sheldon some `Star Trek memorabilia, but she is irked when they refuse to play with the toys in case their value is diminished by removing them from the packaging.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Stephen Hawking comes to lecture at the university for a week, Wolowitz is given the task of maintaining his wheelchair, making Sheldon green with envy. When Sheldon begs Wolowitz to introduce him to his hero Hawking, Wolowitz takes full advantage and only agrees after making Sheldon carry out a series of humiliating tasks, including doing his laundry, cleaning his room, and taking his mother dress shopping.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon and Koothrappali throw a classy bachelor party for Wolowitz, during which some stories about the future bridegrooms colourful sexual history are accidentally revealed. When Bernadette hears about it, she becomes upset and threatens to call off the wedding. Can Wolowitz win her over with a convincing explanation and a heartfelt apology to get the nuptials back on track?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When NASA tells a nervous Wolowitz that his space mission has been rescheduled, he tries to use the wedding as an excuse not to go, but hes forced to agree when he realises that Bernadettes father only likes him because hes an astronaut. Amy develops a cunning psychological plan to bring Sheldon closer, and Penny is surprised by a proposal at an inappropriate moment.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the last episode of season five, Bernadette decides that she wants to be married to Wolowitz before his NASA space mission takes place. They scramble to organise a wedding ceremony at City Hall in the short time available, but their plans go awry. Can their friends step in to find an ordained minister and a romantic wedding venue before its too late?

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: It is March and Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn greet the long-awaited arrival of spring. It is time to bring in the daffodil harvest. During its heyday in the early 20th century, the Tamar Valley was the largest producer of early daffodils in Britain - the result of the regions mild climate combined with the arrival of a railway, which meant produce could be delivered to towns and cities across Britain within hours of bei.

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: Marcus develops plans to create a orchard and bring in the perfect eating apple for his favourite dessert, the classic tarte Tatin. A trip to Kent introduces him to the wide range of heritage apples available in the UK, as well as giving him some tips and tricks on how to grow his own. Back at the farm, he gets a lesson on attracting pollinators, and discovers there is a whole world of edible flowers. He also cooks up some incredible Indian-style chutneys with a glut from the kitchen garden.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: It is April and the fishing season has arrived - a time when Devons `fisherman-farmers went to sea. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn go to sea in an Edwardian trawler, hastily finishing repairs before setting sail. They master the singing of sea shanties as well as steering a wind-powered vessel and casting a net the old-fashioned way - but will they catch any fish?

programme.desc: Dave revamps a tired bathroom in Matt and Jos quirky new home. They all muck in to tear out the toilet and basin before adding a stylish new suite.

programme.desc: Lady Agnes catches the eye of charismatic American millionaire Caspar Landry but, as she and Sir Hallam look to their future, a tragic turn of events leaves their promised dreams - and a close family member - in jeopardy. Meanwhile, downstairs, Mrs Thackeray makes a momentous decision, which throws the running of the house into chaos, and Blanche and Mr Amanjit continue to clash.

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Blanche comes face to face with her former lover when a scandalous novel is published and, when the passion between the pair reignites, begins to consider a life away from Eaton Place. Downstairs, Beryls decision to take a stand causes serious ructions to the running of the house until a familiar face offers wise words.

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: You Could Be Next
programme.desc: Most people think of deepfakes as manipulated video clips of world leaders, designed to create confusion, but the most pressing threat of deepfakes is not politics, but porn. Research shows around 96 per cent of deepfake videos are pornographic, with almost 100 per cent of them involving non-consenting women. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Situated on the eastern coast of the Isle of Man, the village of Laxey is known today as a tourist destination, but as archaeologist Ben Robinson finds out, it wasnt the beaches and the mountains that attracted Victorian tourists, but an engineering marvel - the biggest waterwheel in the world.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson takes us to the quiet village of Ravenglass, tucked away on the edge of the Lake Districts National Park, where he finds out about its strategic importance to the Roman empire. Ben explores the village and its surrounding area to uncover the remarkable evidence of its Roman past: unearthing Roman pottery, locating the earthworks of its fort and marvelling at one the best surviving examples of a Roman military bathhouse anywhere in Britain.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist, Ben Robinson visits the east coast village of Flamborough which sits on a rugged headland sticking out into the North Sea. A largely unassuming village today, it was once effectively cut off from the rest of the country by a five-metre-deep, two-and-a-half-mile long man-made dyke, re-enforced and fortified with local chalk. As Ben discovers when he visits two of the villages most prominent structures, chalk plays a large part in the history of Flamborough.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and discovers that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben finds a village green that was once a thriving port with big ships and surrounded by a thriving village. He visits the church and meets Matt Champion, an expert in medieval graffiti who has found some incredible links to past villagers.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 thats been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Ferdinand Porsche was one of the most influential engineers in Nazi Germany. By inventing the most powerful war machines, he created the basis for the muscle cars that made his name famous.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: In April 1945, the Nazis organise one of the final acts of the Third Reich, a concert by the Berlin Philharmonic, including the last scene of the opera Die Gotterdammerung, which features a suicide at its centre. Its a clear sign that Hitler and many of his supporters are going to end it all.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona investigates the history of the Manchester Ship Canal which links the inland city to the River Mersey and the Irish Sea. Treasures include an opera singers perfume bottle, a travelling magicians box of tricks and an early animation machine.

programme.desc: On Cromer pier in Norfolk, treasures turning up include a letter from John Lennon, a contraption marked `Certain Death, and some valuable Swedish glass bought at a church sale for just 50 pence.

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwalls Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur. Ben discovers Tintagel was potentially more important than London a thousand years ago, based on recent evidence unearthed at an archaeological dig overseen by English Heritage. It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits a small village in Northumberland to find out how the family and village who both share the name Craster have had their lives and fortunes intertwined for over 800 years. The village sits on a unique seam of rock called the Whin Sill and next to the North Sea.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Lying off the North Sea is the unassuming Suffolk village of Orford. Its behind a large shingle spit called Orford Ness. But as archaeologist Ben Robinson unearths, its been at the forefront of defending the British Isles for over 800 years. Today its imposing castle built by Henry II is being conserved by English Heritage. Curator Dr Shelley Garland explains the process to Ben and how theyve devised the best way to preserve its crumbling stonework.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores Parkgate, on the estuary of the River Dee in Cheshire. Today the village is landlocked, cut off from the Dee and the Irish Sea due to the estuarys shifting silt. But 250 years ago, it was a bustling harbour and as Ben finds out from Dr Gillian OBrian, Parkgate was a gateway for thousands of Irish immigrants from the 1620s onwards.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan +4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The untold secret story of war production that shaped the Second World War. Key factories for weapons, vehicles and ships are highlighted, including the American Liberty Ships.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: At the end of the Second World War, British troops are confronted with the true horrors of Nazi crimes when they discover Bergen Belsen concentration camp.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team presents from Eltham Palace, an art deco gem in the leafy suburbs of south London. Jewellery expert Joanna Hardy is dazzled by an exquisite emerald and diamond bracelet made by one of Paris? top design houses.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces previously unscreened finds from two recent venues: Lanhydrock in Cornwall and Bodnant Garden in North Wales. Objects uncovered by the experts include a collection of jewellery made for suffragettes, a rare painting rescued from a skip at Sissinghurst, and a fascinating group of photographs recording the Queens first Christmas broadcast.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
